reference: GATE CS — Computer Networks / Data Link Layer

formulas.name: Ethernet Minimum Frame Size
formulas.plain: Minimum Ethernet frame = 64 bytes (512 bits); maximum payload 1500 bytes, max frame 1518 bytes
formulas.whenToUse: Collision detection sizing and padding small payloads.
formulas.trap: Payloads < 46 bytes are padded so the frame reaches 64 bytes.

formulas.name: Slot Time
formulas.plain: Slot time = 512 bit-times = round-trip + jam; basis of the 2500 m max length
formulas.whenToUse: Reasoning about collision detection window and backoff.
formulas.trap: Slot time scales with speed; gigabit uses carrier extension to keep it.

formulas.name: Binary Exponential Backoff
formulas.plain: After n-th collision wait K slot-times, K chosen uniformly in [0, 2^n - 1], capped at n=10
formulas.whenToUse: Computing expected retransmission delay after collisions.

terms.means: collision number (capped at 10, give up at 16)

formulas.trap: The exponent caps at 10; the attempt count caps at 16.

formulas.name: Transparent Bridge Learning
formulas.plain: A bridge learns which MAC is on which port from source addresses; floods if destination unknown
formulas.whenToUse: Tracing frame forwarding/flooding through a learning bridge.
formulas.trap: Unknown-unicast, broadcast and multicast are flooded to all ports except the arrival port.

formulas.name: Collision vs Broadcast Domain
formulas.latex: \text{switch: each port} = 1\text{ coll. dom},\ \text{whole switch} = 1\text{ bcast dom}
formulas.plain: Hub = one collision domain; switch = one collision domain per port; router separates broadcast domains
formulas.whenToUse: Counting collision/broadcast domains in a topology.

terms.means: splits one switch into multiple broadcast domains

formulas.trap: Bridges/switches do NOT break broadcast domains; only routers (or VLANs) do.
